NPR CEO on Public Media in the Age of Trump
data.description:
Public broadcasting as a whole is facing new challenges, as outlets like PBS and NPR saw their share of federal funding cut by Congress over the summer raising questions about their future. At the center of that debate is NPR CEO Katherine Maher, who was called before an oversight committee earlier this year. She joins Caroline Hyde and Ed Ludlow on "Bloomberg Tech."

7-Eleven Sees Same-Store Fuel Volume Decline in 2Q -- OPIS
data.description:
7-Eleven's same-store retail fuel volume declined by 4.8% year to year in the second quarter of fiscal 2025 though fuel profit margins increased by 2.9%, parent company Seven & i said in financial results for the period ended Aug. 31.

The company's fuel volume underperformed the national average for U.S. gasoline stations, which recorded a 2.6% year-to-year decline in same-store fuel volume June through August, according to OPIS DemandPro.

Same-store dollar sales for store merchandise declined 0.8% year to year but showed a slight improvement in July and August. 7-Eleven's merchandise margin dipped 0.6% year to year and was just above 33%.

Seven & i said that though U.S. existing store merchandise sales were down from the prior year in the first half of fiscal 2025, operating income (before amortization of goodwill) was about double year on year because of improved gross profit margin from adding proprietary products and optimizing labor costs.

Gross profit margin was lower than the previous year because of "soaring raw material prices," the company added.

Same-store traffic declined 5.0% year to year in 2Q but improved in July (-1.9%) and August (-2.5%).

And transactions were larger. The average basket size in 2Q increased by 4.4% year to year and was up 2.7% year to year in July and 2.8% in August.

"The average spending per customer exceeded the previous year due to value offering measures centered on fresh food, but existing store sales were lower than the previous year due to the impact of the decrease in the number of customers," the company said.

Seven & i said 7-Eleven made progress on several key initiatives:


-- The retail network opened 56 new stores in the first half of the fiscal year and is expected to open another 69 in the second half for a total of 125 by the end of the fiscal year. The new stores perform better than legacy stores.

-- 7-Eleven opened 14 new restaurants in the first half and is expected to open 36 more in the second half for a total of 50 in fiscal 2025.

-- 7-Eleven launched 118 new private-label items in the first half and is expected to add at least another 82 items to total more than 200 by fiscal year end.

The chain also is expanding the 7NOW delivery program, after seeing 21.3% in same-store sales growth in the first half, with an average basket size of $15.40. The average delivery time was about 28 minutes, the company said.

7NOW recorded $755 million in sales in fiscal 2024 and sales are projected to be $1 billion in fiscal 2025.


This content was created by Oil Price Information Service, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. OPIS is run independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.


-- Reporting by Donna Harris, dharris@opisnet.com; Editing by Michael Kelly, mkelly@opisnet.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

10-09-25 1612ET

Texas Agency to Issue $1.77 Billion in Bonds for Transportation
data.description:
By Paulo Trevisani


The Texas Transportation Commission is seeking to raise $1.77 billion through municipal bonds to fund state highway projects, while also refunding outstanding debt.

The General Obligation Mobility Fund and Refunding Bonds, Series 2025, will finance the construction, acquisition and expansion of state highway and public transportation projects, according to a preliminary statement published Thursday on MuniOs. The bonds are part of the state's Mobility Fund financing program.

The tax-exempt bonds mature between 2026 and 2044. Coupons and yields are yet to be set.

The bonds are backed by revenues deposited in the Mobility Fund on a first-lien basis. The Mobility Fund's dedicated revenues totaled $528.8 million in fiscal 2024, derived primarily from driver license, vehicle inspection and certificate of title fees.

Pricing is preliminarily set for Oct. 21, with closing on Nov. 6.

The offering have been rated AAA by Fitch and Aaa by Moody's.

Loop Capital Markets and Jefferies are leading underwriters.


Write to Paulo Trevisani at paulo.trevisani@wsj.com

(END) Dow Jones Newswires

10-09-25 1550ET

Evommune, Biotech Focused on Inflammatory Diseases, Files for IPO
data.description:
By Josh Beckerman


Evommune, which is developing drugs targeting the sources of chronic inflammatory diseases, filed for an initial public offering.

The Palo Alto, Calif., company is seeking a New York Stock Exchange listing under the ticker symbol EVMN.

Evommune announced $115 million of Series C financing co-led by RA Capital Management and Sectoral Asset Management in October 2024.

Top shareholders include Pivotal bioVenture Partners and EQT Life Sciences, which each own a 16.3% stake, according to the clinical-stage biotechnology company's filing.

In September, the company reported favorable data from a Phase 2 trial of EVO756 in adults with chronic inducible urticaria.

A portion of the net proceeds will go to fund development of EVO756, which the company describes as its most advanced clinical-stage product candidate. Money will also be used for additional research and development projects.


Write to Josh Beckerman at josh.beckerman@wsj.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

Sector Update: Energy
Corn Futures Sink as Day Passes With No WASDE - Daily Grain Highlights
data.description:
By Kirk Maltais


--Corn for December delivery fell 0.8%, to $4.18 1/2 a bushel, on the Chicago Board of Trade Thursday, with traders mostly taking the path of least resistance by selling corn and soybeans in the absence of the scheduled October WASDE report.

--Soybeans for November delivery fell 0.8%, to $10.21 3/4 a bushel.

--Wheat for December delivery rose 0.2%, to $5.08 a bushel.


HIGHLIGHTS


Missing Milestone: The October WASDE report was due from the USDA, but didn't come because of the continuing government shutdown. "That leaves the market in the dark on what USDA would have done with its production estimates as the crop matures and as harvest gained momentum," said Arlan Suderman of StoneX in a note. The October report is often an important one to determine crop health as harvesting rolls on, and would move markets.

Less on the Ground: Russia's agricultural minister says the country is expected to seed 2% less wheat this marketing year than it did in the last one, counter to expectations of more wheat being planted. Russian farmers appear to be choosing to plant more oilseeds, said AgResource in a note, adding that farmers are expected to seed 8.5% more oilseed crops including soybeans, due to higher profit margins versus wheat.


INSIGHT


Regaining Strength: Treasury yields and the dollar climbed as the government shutdown wears on. The weekly jobless claims report was delayed for the second consecutive week. Economists surveyed by The Wall Street Journal expected 230,000 new claims, which would keep the layoffs gauge within range. For agriculture, a stronger dollar generally makes exports less competitive versus other origins.

Change Complete: As of September, the climate has shifted from being neutral to being a La Nina, NOAA's Climate Prediction Center said. The condition is expected to remain until early next year, according to NOAA, potentially going back to a neutral condition between January and March 2026. La Nina tends to bring colder and wetter conditions to the Corn Belt states in the winter, while conditions tend to be drier and warmer in the southern U.S. For farmers, this may mean adequate snow covering for winter wheat--and potentially strong soil moisture come the spring 2026 planting season.


AHEAD


Note: The government shutdown may affect the timing of USDA reports and data releases.

--The CFTC is scheduled to release its weekly Commitment of Traders report at 3:30 p.m. ET Friday.

--The USDA is scheduled to release its weekly Grain Export Inspections report at 11 a.m. ET Tuesday.

--The USDA is scheduled to release its weekly Crop Progress report at 4 p.m. ET Tuesday.


--Paulo Trevisani contributed to this article.


Write to Kirk Maltais at kirk.maltais@wsj.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

10-09-25 1527ET

Tata Consultancy Services Limited acquired ListEngage, Inc. for $72.8 million.
data.description:
Tata Consultancy Services Limited (NSEI:TCS) acquired ListEngage, Inc. for $72.8 million on October 9, 2025. A cash consideration of $72.8 million will be paid by Tata Consultancy Services Limited. As part of consideration, $72.8 million is paid towards common equity of ListEngage, Inc.

For the period ending December 31, 2024, ListEngage, Inc. reported total revenue of $24.3 million.

The transaction has been approved by the board of Tata Consultancy Services Limited.
Tata Consultancy Services Limited (NSEI:TCS) completed the acquisition of ListEngage, Inc. on October 9, 2025.

YouTuber Kinigra Deon on How 'New Hollywood' is Redefining Media
data.description:
YouTuber Kinigra Deon isn't just making content. She is creating cinematic, long-form stories for the internet age. With Hollywood-level production and a creator's agility, Deon works to redefine what resonates on the digital screen while scaling, monetizing and building loyal audiences. She joins Carol Massar and Tim Stenovec on 'Bloomberg Businessweek Daily' from Bloomberg Screentime to discuss her digital platform, recent production deal with Kevin Hart, and how YouTubers and content creators are redefining the boundary between traditional media and the ever-growing digital world.

Bloomberg videos, provided by MT Newswires
